skill.name: openai-docs-skill
skill.description: Query the OpenAI developer documentation via the OpenAI Docs MCP server using CLI (curl/jq). Use whenever a task involves the OpenAI API (Responses, Chat Completions, Realtime, etc.), OpenAI SDKs, ChatGPT Apps SDK, Codex, MCP integrations, endpoint schemas, parameters, limits, or migrations and you need up-to-date official guidance.
skill.summary: Query official OpenAI developer docs via MCP server CLI wrapper
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate documentation access skill. Static analyzer flagged 44 potential issues, but evaluation confirms all are false positives. The script uses jq for safe JSON construction, makes read-only HTTP requests to OpenAI's official MCP server, and performs standard filesystem checks. No malicious intent or security vulnerabilities identified.

critical_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
critical_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:23. Line 3 is YAML frontmatter (description field), line 23 is a bash command example. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: Analysis of the actual file content confirms these lines contain documentation and bash examples, not cryptographic algorithms. Scanner appears to have false-positive triggered by 'MCP' keyword.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Script
medium_findings.description: Script uses jq for JSON construction. While this involves shell commands, arguments are properly sanitized using --arg and --argjson flags which prevent injection. This is standard practice for CLI tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detected but legitimate use case. jq with --arg flags is safe JSON construction, not command injection vulnerability.

low_findings.title: HTTP Requests to External Endpoint
low_findings.description: Script makes HTTP requests to https://developers.openai.com/mcp which is OpenAI's official documentation MCP server. This is the intended functionality of the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate network access to known OpenAI documentation endpoint. Expected behavior for a documentation tool.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Command Checks
low_findings.description: Script uses command -v to verify required tools (curl, jq) are installed. Standard shell practice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard filesystem check for command availability, not file access vulnerability.

content.value_statement: Get up-to-date OpenAI API documentation directly in your AI workflow. This skill queries the official OpenAI Docs MCP server to fetch accurate, current references for all OpenAI APIs, SDKs, and tools.
actual_capabilities: Search OpenAI developer documentation via MCP server
Fetch specific doc pages or sections by URL with optional anchor
List available documentation topics and categories
Retrieve OpenAPI specs for OpenAI endpoints
Get code examples in multiple programming languages
Query ChatGPT Apps SDK and Codex documentation
limitations: Requires curl and jq to be installed on the system
Needs network access to communicate with OpenAI MCP server
Cannot modify or write to external systems - read-only documentation access
Depends on OpenAI MCP server availability

use_cases.title: API Implementation
use_cases.description: When implementing OpenAI API features, quickly lookup parameter schemas, rate limits, and response formats

use_cases.title: Migration Assistance
use_cases.description: Get step-by-step migration guides when moving between OpenAI API versions or endpoints

use_cases.title: SDK Reference
use_cases.description: Access SDK-specific documentation for Python, Node.js, and other supported languages

prompt_templates.title: Basic API Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the openai-docs-skill to find documentation about the [Responses API / Chat Completions API / Realtime API]. Show me the endpoint URL, request parameters, and response format.

prompt_templates.title: Parameter Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the [temperature, max_tokens, stream] parameter in the OpenAI docs. Include the parameter description, valid values, and default settings.

prompt_templates.title: Migration Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the migration guide for moving from [Chat Completions to Responses API]. What are the key differences and what code changes are needed?

prompt_templates.title: Code Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Get a code example for calling the [ Assistants API ] using the [ Python / Node.js ] SDK. Include authentication setup and basic usage.

output_examples.output: Returns JSON with 5 search results from OpenAI docs, each containing title, URL, and relevance score
Returns markdown content of the migration guide with step-by-step instructions and code samples
Returns code samples in Python and JavaScript for calling the Responses API endpoint

best_practices: Always include the source URL in your response when using docs from this skill
Use specific search queries to get more relevant results
Check the doc URL timestamp to confirm you have current information
Use the anchor parameter to link directly to specific sections
anti_patterns: Relying on memory rather than checking official docs for current parameters
Using outdated tutorials instead of official documentation
Ignoring rate limits and error handling documented in the docs
Not verifying API versions match the documentation you are reading

faq.question: How do I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use the CLI wrapper at scripts/openai-docs-mcp.sh with subcommands like search, fetch, list, or openapi. The skill will automatically query OpenAI's official documentation MCP server.

faq.question: What documentation sources does this access?
faq.answer: This skill connects to OpenAI's official MCP server at developers.openai.com/mcp, providing access to all public OpenAI API documentation, SDK guides, and migration docs.

faq.question: Do I need any dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires curl and jq to be installed on your system. These are used to make HTTP requests and process JSON responses.

faq.question: Can this skill make API calls to OpenAI?
faq.answer: No, this skill only accesses documentation. It does not make calls to OpenAI's chat or completion APIs. It queries the documentation MCP server only.

faq.question: How often is the documentation updated?
faq.answer: The skill fetches live documentation from OpenAI's servers, so you always get the current version. Check the doc URL or look for version info in the content.

faq.question: Can I get code examples in specific languages?
faq.answer: Yes, use the openapi subcommand with language parameters. For example: openapi <url> python,javascript code-only will return code samples in both languages.
